channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Perplexity extension on Chrome Web Store tracked searches
item.description: A malicious extension in the Chrome Web Store is masquerading as the Perplexity AI answer engine, intercepting search traffic and collecting browsing information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Silent Swap Crypto Clipper Uses Fake Google Notes Extension to Replace Wallet Addresses
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged an active browser extension campaign that is designed to steal cryptocurrency by stealthily replacing wallet addresses when unsuspecting users initiate a transac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GuardFall Exposes Open-Source AI Coding Agents to Decades-Old Shell Injection Risks
item.description:
The safety check that is supposed to stop an AI coding agent from running a dangerous command can be walked straight past using a shell trick that has been public for decades.
New research from Adver...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Lessons from the Underground: How to Combat Business Email Compromise
item.description: Business Email Compromise is more than an email scam. It's a coordinated operation involving compromised accounts, financial research, and cash-out networks. Flare explores how underground forums reve...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 282 iOS AI Apps Leak API Keys and Open AI Proxy Access in Network Traffic Study
item.description:
Researchers tested 444 AI chatbot apps for iPhone and found that 282 of them, nearly two-thirds, exposed paid AI access through their network traffic.
In many cases, the path in was visible just by w...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed. Threat actor a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
item.description: An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver and Djinn Steal...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Insurance giant Aflac discloses data breach after subsidiary hack
item.description: American insurance giant Aflac has disclosed a new data breach after attackers breached its Japan subsidiary's systems and stole personal and bank account information. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft adds smarter bot protection to Teams meetings
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new Teams admin policy that allows organizers to prevent third-party bots from joining meetings without approval. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Kali Linux 2026.2 released with 9 new tools, NetHunter updates
item.description: Kali Linux 2026.2, the second release of the year, is now available for download, featuring 9 new tools and numerous Kali NetHunter improvements. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Blackfield ransomware asks Nidec Corporation for $2 million ransom
item.description: The Blackfield ransomware gang is asking for a $2 million ransom from Nidec Corporation, a large Japanese manufacturer of electronic components for automotive and computing applications. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
item.description:
Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network.
An attacker within wireless ra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs
item.description: CISA confirmed on Monday that ransomware gangs are now exploiting a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation vulnerability, dubbed BlueHammer, that has previously been abused in zero-day attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
item.description: Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI browsers an...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
item.description:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.
The flaw, tracked...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
item.description:
A critical security flaw impacting Oracle E-Business Suite has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to Defused Cyber.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS score: 9.8), ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
item.description: Nissan is warning that it suffered a data breach affecting current and former employees after threat actors exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerability in data theft attacks previously linked to the ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
item.description: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) says the ShinyHunters extortion group stole only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files after breaching its systems ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WhatsApp rolls out usernames to help users hide their phone number
item.description: WhatsApp is finally allowing users to reserve usernames, a privacy feature that lets them hide their phone numbers from people not in their contact list. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft extends Windows Server 2022 hotpatching until October 2027
item.description: Microsoft has extended Windows Server 2022 hotpatching until October 2027, one year after the mainstream end date of October 2026. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: WhatsApp is Finally Getting Usernames to Help Keep Phone Numbers Private
item.description:
WhatsApp on Monday officially announced the start of global reservations of usernames with an aim to protect the privacy of more than three billion users on the messaging platform.
The optional featu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious Perplexity Chrome Extension Intercepted Searches and Address Bar Input
item.description: Microsoft has found a malicious Chrome extension that posed as the AI search engine Perplexity and quietly logged what people searched for. It routed every query and every character typed into the add...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apple Patches 30+ iOS, macOS, Safari Flaws, Including AI-Discovered WebKit Bugs
item.description: Apple on Monday released security updates for iOS, macOS, and the Safari web browser to address over three dozen flaws, including four vulnerabilities in WebKit that were discovered using artificial i...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: U.S. offers $10 million for hackers targeting WhatsApp, Signal users
item.description: The U.S. Department of State is offering up to $10 million for information that helps identify or locate members of the UNC5792 and UNC4221 hacker groups, which are linked to Russia's intelligence and...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mustang Panda Uses Zoho WorkDrive as Command Channel in Indian Government Attacks
item.description: The China-aligned espionage group Mustang Panda is running two campaigns against the Indian government and hydropower targets, deploying new malware and turning a legitimate cloud service into its com...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Kernel Flaws, AI Malware Tricks, Turla Backdoor, Infostealers and More
item.description:
This week was a reminder that attackers do not always need big tricks. One small mistake, one old access path, one missed patch, and suddenly the door is open.
The noise is not all noise, either. For...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
item.description: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, and take action across enterprise systems. Token Security explains why governing these privileged identities is becoming essential for enterprise security...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Critical SimpleHelp flaw exploited to deploy new stealer malware
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a recently disclosed critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-48558) in SimpleHelp to deploy Djinn Stealer, a previously undocumented cross-platform information stealer targeting Window...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers now exploit critical Oracle E-Business flaw in attacks
item.description: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817) in the Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) financial application, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 236,000 DCloud Uni-App Sites Used in Crypto Scams, Phishing, and Wallet Drainers
item.description: New findings unearthed by Infoblox show that more than 236,000 websites are using investment scam templates built using a legitimate Chinese open-source, cross-platform application development framewo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
